channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Blocked drains on either side of the road, 2nd June
item.description:
Each time it rains there is always a build of water on both sides of the road that should have disappeared into the drains but remains for days after
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 44B, Newlands Park, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
See attached photos. Not marked up for attention

item.title: Flooded pavement, 2nd June
item.description:
Every time it rains this pavement is flooded whereby you cannot even walk on that side of the road.assuming drains need unblocking

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brookmill Road, Deptford, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lights stuck on red in both directions causing build up of traffic and disrupting the buses, 2nd June
item.description:
Temporary traffic lights are stuck on red in both directions and causing congestion and a potential danger due to the bend in the road being obscured by parked cars and a narrow road.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 107, Leesons Hill, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rain water road drains incorrect height, 2nd June
item.description:
Bridge road, oldfield road, Herbert road all suffer from standing water, especially near dropped kerbs. Ever since road resurfacing works, the storm drains are now sitting higher than adjacent road surface levels- rain water does not flow or drain into the drainage because of poor installation by the contractor. The road surfaces are beginning to degrade where the standing water sits and will not last long, wasting our council tax funds on poor quality works.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bridge Road, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 2 potholes opposite 60 Petts Wood Road, 2nd June
item.description:
See photos Not marked up for attention

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 60, Petts Wood Road, Poverest, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Potholes, 2nd June
item.description:
Hello my case number was 9529225 and I showed pictures but they said it’s private property which in these photo's I have put up it’s the main road I wasn't clear before that was my mistake but it’s on the main road and its an issue for cars and people it’s been here since I’ve moved here which was almost 3 years ago now hope you consider this thanks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Charlieville Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Same oro kW, 2nd June
item.description:
Same problem every year - the weeds again encroaching our property. Strangled our Sky box yet again, so no service. My husband is partially disabled and relies on it. Very frustrating. Please action as a matter of urgency. Thank you Linda Holland

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Taunton Close, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocked gully pit blocked on Crystal Palace Parade, 2nd June
item.description:
Blocked gully pit adding to wide and deep flooding on Crystal Palace Parade. Photo of gully pit attached from yesterday, and before today's rain and can be seen to be full of debris. Ponding today after rain does not drain quickly and stays flooded for hours. This gully pit to be cleaned asap as the nearby gully pit next to the Crystal Palace Subway is known to be partially blocked and also floods this area. Having to gullies partially blocked is adding to severe flooding at the bus station where many buses terminate their routes.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crystal Palace Parade, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Willesden lane by Kilburn high road, 2nd June
item.description:
By the bus stop

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Willesden Lane, Kilburn, London

item.title: Back with bins, 2nd June
item.description:
Bins are back again requiring me to squeeze past. Let’s see how long they stay on the pavement this time two months three months four months they manage two days which was joyful!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 8, Greenside, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Traffic bollard in state of disrepair, 2nd June
item.description:
This bollard requires sanding and painting back to black and white to ensure drivers are prepared for the winter and night time drivers. It is a small job. Junction of days lane and Ashcroft Avenue.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ashcroft Avenue, Blackfen, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The roundabout at the bottom of Gravel hill is flooded, 2nd June
item.description:
Due to the heavy over night rain, the roundabout is flooded and requires urgent attention. This matter has been reported a few times and still not been resolved. When the area was resurfaced a few months ago, the work to improve the drainage on the roundabout could have been arranged and completed within the same time frame.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bourne Road, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned vehicle (caravan), 2nd June
item.description:
Blocking road

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 205, High Street, Poverest, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Nw6 1xn, 2nd June
item.description:
Dear Camden Council, I am the director of a restaurant on West End Lane, NW6 1XN And I am writing to request a review of the bus stop shelter and bench located directly outside my premises. To be clear, I am not requesting the removal of the bus stop itself. I would simply like the Council and Transport for London to consider replacing the current shelter and bench with a simple pole-style bus stop, similar to other bus stops in the area. The current arrangement is having a significant impact on my business. During rainy weather, which is very common throughout the year, large numbers of passengers gather under the shelter directly in front of the restaurant. This frequently obscures the frontage, signage and entrance of the premises, making it difficult for pedestrians to see the restaurant from the street. As a small independent business, visibility is extremely important. When groups of people are waiting under the shelter, the restaurant can become almost completely hidden from view. This affects customer footfall, reduces the visibility of the business and has a negative impact on trade. The shelter and bench also restrict the effective use of the frontage and limit opportunities to improve the public-facing appearance of the premises. A simpler bus stop design would continue to serve passengers while reducing the impact on neighbouring businesses. I pay substantial business rates each year and remain committed to investing in my business and contributing to the local economy. I would therefore be grateful if the Council could review this matter with Transport for London and consider whether a less intrusive bus stop arrangement could be provided at this location. I have attached photographs showing how the restaurant frontage becomes obscured when passengers gather under the shelter, particularly during wet weather. Thank you for your time and consideration. I would appreciate any guidance or assistance you can provide and look forward to hearing from you. Kind regards Nasim Amiri 245 west end lane Nw6 1xn Bibi Persian kitchen 07511106222
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 127, West End Lane, Kilburn, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road badly worn, 2nd June
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 72-82, Rosebery Avenue, Clerkenwell, Greater London
